name: Post-sphaleron baryogenesis
summary: Generates the baryon asymmetry below the electroweak phase transition (T < T_EWPT ~ 100 GeV), after sphaleron processes have decoupled, by direct out-of-equilibrium decays of a TeV-scale baryon-number-violating singlet scalar. Connects to neutron-antineutron oscillations (Delta B = 2) and color-sextet scalar phenomenology. Provides a leptogenesis-independent route to BAU testable at LHC and via n-nbar searches.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces eta ~ 6e-10 with a TeV-scale singlet decaying to color-sextet diquarks. Generates Delta B = 2 operators that drive observable n-nbar oscillations - directly testable at upcoming European Spallation Source NNBAR and tests at PSI.

touches_benchmarks.note: Mechanism requires Delta B = 2 (not Delta B = 1) operators, suppressing proton decay relative to n-nbar oscillation. Consistent with Super-K p-decay bounds while predicting accessible n-nbar.

introduces.note: Singlet mass, Yukawa couplings, CP phases, color-sextet mass.

history: Babu and Mohapatra (2006), 'Post-sphaleron baryogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 97, 131301, arXiv:hep-ph/0606144. Refined upper bound on n-nbar oscillation time: Babu-Dev-Mohapatra (2013), 'Post-sphaleron baryogenesis and an upper limit on the neutron-antineutron oscillation time', Phys. Rev. D 87, 115019, arXiv:1303.6918. ESS NNBAR experiment (2025+) directly tests the prediction.

references.citation: Babu and Mohapatra, 'Post-sphaleron baryogenesis', Phys. Rev. Lett. 97, 131301 (2006)
Babu, Dev, Mohapatra, 'Post-sphaleron baryogenesis and an upper limit on the neutron-antineutron oscillation time', Phys. Rev. D 87, 115019 (2013)
